message:
fix(freeze): resolve vstack/real-stack mismatch at control flow boundaries

This fix addresses a bug where optional chaining with computed properties
(e.g., `def.shape?.[k]`) produced incorrect results in freeze mode.

Root cause: DUP opcodes operating in vstack mode didn't modify the real
stack, but the CFG analysis assumed they did. This caused base_stack_depth
to be incorrect at block boundaries, leading to wrong stack slot references.

Changes:
1. Add materializeVStack() before control flow opcodes (if_false, if_true,
   goto) in loop context to ensure real stack matches CFG expectations

2. Fix get_loc_check and get_var to use base_stack_depth tracking instead
   of vpush("stack[sp-1]") to prevent double-tracking when pushing to both
   real stack AND vstack

3. Fix block terminator to skip materializing entries that are references
   to existing stack values ("stack[sp - N]") since they're already on the
   real stack

Tests:
- All 10 Zod tests now pass (previously failing with schema errors)
- All 27 RxJS tests still pass (no regression)
- Added test_optional_chaining_array.js regression test
